events.title: Little Artists
events.description: Drop-in program. A creative art program where children explore paint, collage, natural materials, and lose parts. Focus is on creativity and self-expression while building fine motor skills. This program is co-sponsored with Agincourt Community Services Association (ACSA).

events.title: EarlyOn Circle Time
events.description: In partnership with the York West EarlyON Child and Family Centre, our program offers a range of activities including stories, songs, rhymes, and more, providing plenty of opportunities for children to learn and develop new skills in an enjoyable environment. For children ages 0 to 6 and their caregivers.

events.title: LEGO Hour
events.description: Enjoy an hour of building with our various blocks! All children are welcome.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers.   Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act, sections 20(a), 20(c), and 20(d). The information will only be used for event and program registration, delivery and evaluation. Questions about how the library handles your information can be directed to Answerline

events.title: EarlyON Play & Learn
events.description: Join Macaulay Centres for Children and EarlyON Child and Family Centre for songs, rhymes, stories, gross and fine, motor activities and many more with their parent or caregiver. Walk-ins are welcome but space is limited.

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us Tuesday mornings from 10:00 AM to 10:30 AM for a half-hour of high-energy fun! We will be reading great books, singing our favorite tunes, and dancing to action songs. Parents and caregivers are encouraged to join in on the fun as we bring you our very best. This is a drop-in program, so no registration is needed unless you are bringing a group of 5 or more children. Please note: Toddler Time will be paused on Tuesday, June 23, 2026, for our Toddler Time Graduation Ceremony.

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children, 19 months to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers. Drop in.

events.title: L'Heure du conte / French Storytime
events.description: Rejoignez-nous pour des histoires, des chansons et des comptines en français pour les enfants âgés de 0 à 5 ans, accompagnés de leurs parents et responsables. Les familles sont invitées à rester et à jouer pour 30 minutes supplémentaires après la conclusion du programme. Enjoy stories, songs and rhymes in French at a staff-led story time for children age 0-5 with their parents and caregivers. Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children 19 months to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers. Toddler Time runs from  10:30  to 11:00 .

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities for children aged 18 months-3 years with their parents or caregivers. Drop-in.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Bouncing and tickling rhymes, songs and stories for babies from birth to 18 months with their parents and caregivers. Followed by play time for babies with caregivers. Drop-in only. 10:30-11:30

events.title: Rainbow Day: Baby/Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for a special Rainbow Storytime where we celebrate all children and families! The Storytime will include a range of inclusive books, stories, songs and rhymes that foster acceptance, embrace diversity, and help get kids ready for reading! This program is suitable for children ​3 years and under with a parent or caregiver. Drop-in. Baby/Toddler Time runs from 10:30am   to  11:00am . Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play, from 11:00am  to 11:30am.

events.title: Toddler Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children, 19 months to 3 years, and their parents or caregivers.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Join us for bouncing rhymes, songs and stories in this program for babies, birth to 18 months, and their parents or caregivers. Baby Time runs from 11am to 11:30am. Children, parents and caregivers are invited to Stay and Play, from 11:30am to 12pm. *On June 9th , please join us after the program from 11:30am – 12:30pm for a Q&A with a Toronto Public Health Nurse on topics related to healthy parenting.

events.title: EarlyON Family Resource Program
events.description: Learn about quality community resources for young families, enjoy a circle time for the young ones, and get some nutritious snacks. Suitable for families with children birth to six years old.  In partnership with EarlyON Child and Family Centre of Regent Park Community Health Centre.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Join us for bouncing rhymes, songs and stories in this program for babies, birth to 18 months, and their parents or caregivers. Baby Time runs from 1pm   to  2pm .

events.title: LEGO Free Play
events.description: Do you love to build and to create?  Check out Yorkville's LEGO program!  Have fun, meet friends, and build something awesome in a relaxed drop-in environment. For children ages 4 and up.  No registration is required.  Building toys for younger children are available in the Children's Department.

events.title: LEGO Free-play!
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace.   Drop-In, No registration required.

events.title: Colour by Number
events.description: Colour by Numbers is an interactive colouring activity. It is designed to improve your number recognition skills as you have fun with colours. Join us in this relaxing program and feel your artistic spirit. The most important thing is you do not have to overthink, just do it. All kids. Drop-in, spaces are limited. No registration required.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Drop-in program. A relaxed play program with circle time, process based art, building toys, books, and group activities. Families connect, play together, and build community. This program is co-sponsored with ACSA.

events.title: Family Time at the Park
events.description: Drop in and us at Appletree Market in June Rowlands Park in the main market area for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 4:30  to 5pm . Weather permitting!

events.title: Nature Explorers
events.description: Drop-in program. Explore spring through outdoor play and nature-based activities. Children may plant seeds, touch leaves, soil, water, sand, go on simple scavenger hunts, and create nature-inspired open ended art. Dress for outdoor play when possible. This program is co-sponsored with ACSA.

events.title: Philippe Flahaut: Sous le soleil de l’été

For ages 0-12.

events.title: Fictional Character Bingo and TD Summer Reading Club Draw
events.description: Let's finish off this summer with a fun round of Bingo! This time, we'll be doing popular fictional characters in lieu of numbers. Have you read lots of stories? Maybe you can guess who they are too! Prizes will be given out for Bingo winners! Also, if you've completed the TD Summer Reading Club challenge of finishing 12 books, we'll be doing a draw for special prizes at the end of the program. Drop-in!

events.title: Yoga Storytime with Appleseed Yoga
events.description: ​Join us for an interactive, movement-based program where we read stories and make them come alive with our bodies! Appleseed Yoga for Kids will deliver inspiring stories that get the whole family moving. Class begins and ends with song and breathwork and is filled with pages of creativity and activity. Kids and families can sit quietly and listen, do partial poses or make up their own, following our lead. It's ALL yoga! No experience, mats, or materials required. Drop-in with limited space. All

events.title: Wonder Workshop - Pollution Challenge
events.description: Do you ever think about the impact of one little person or one tiny thing on the planet? When one pollutant becomes many, the planet hurts. But when one person becomes many, positive change can happen. In this workshop, you will design and build your own creature or machine that will help keep our precious planet clean! Plus, enter a prize draw for a chance to win an awesome book.  For kids ages 4 to 8. Space is limited. Registration begins June 1st. Information Collection Notice: The perso

events.description: For ages 6 to 12.  Children under the age of 6 may attend but must be accompanied by a parent or caregiver.   Dive under the sea and celebrate the end of the summer with a game of ocean-themed BINGO! Kids will enjoy multiple rounds of play with underwater-themed cards, friendly competition and chances to win fun book prizes each round.

events.description: Take a trip around our solar system without leaving the comfort of planet Earth. Explore the strange landscapes of the inner and outer planets and their moons. We've even included Pluto! Learn how our understanding of these worlds has evolved since their discovery. In our exhibit you'll find original art, novels, short stories and more set on these fantastic celestial bodies. This exhibit can be viewed during the Merril Collection's regular hours. events.title: Crafturday
events.description: Join us for a day of fun and crafts. Partake in a new and engaging craft every Saturday from 9:30am - 4:30pm. Drop-in and self-serve. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers.

events.description: Let's get moving! Join Guyanese-born Canadian author Yolanda T. Marshall for a lively, interactive storytelling program as she reads from her Caribbean-inspired picture books followed by Soca music. Please note this program will be in place of our regular Saturday Family Storytime.

events.title: Saturday Craft
events.description: To celebrate your Saturday mornings, join us for a fun craft! Supplies are limited. First come first served.

events.description: When each of the umbrellas he brings back to his village disappears, Kiri Mama devises a plan to track down the thief. Ages 0-12 Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: Builder's Club

events.title: 9th Annual Community Book Share
events.description: Join us to promote literacy and recycling in your neighbourhood! Bring your gently used books, CDs and DVDs and take some gently used items home. Lots of free books! Donations in good condition are welcome, but not necessary. Feel free to drop off items any time on or before the event. Come out for coffee and meet your neighbours, library staff, and volunteers.

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: 3D Printing Demo - All Ages
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL's Digital Innovation Hubs. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.title: Explorum Workshop: Kaléidescope / Kaleidescope (Drop-in)
events.description: Qu’est-ce qu’un kaléidoscope ? Une illusion d’optique ? De la magie ? De l’art ? En fait c’est un peu de tout ça. C’est un instrument développé il y a plus de 200 ans qui utilise la réflexion de la lumière sur des miroirs pour créer des motifs répétitifs envoutants. Dans cet atelier, les enfants feront leur propre kaléidoscope et seront exposés aux termes de réflexion et réfraction de lumière tout en étant captivés par la physique des scintillements des motifs sur les surfaces réfléchissantes. events.title: "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" with Author Catherine Little
events.description: On a beautiful spring day, sisters Jane and Cassandra enjoy tea with Mother in the garden. Jane is in the throes of writing a story for Father’s birthday, but too many ideas swirl in her head, and she cannot get started. The Blue Willow pattern on the tea set catches Jane’s attention … will that be the inspiration she’s looking for?​ Catherine Little presents a reading from her picture book " Jane and the Blue Willow Princess " about author Jane Austen, then leads a fun craft with air-dry clay.&

events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. Join us for an introductory lesson and learn about the most common tools. We'll explore the workspace and enhance images while investigating tools such as crop, levels, colour balance and more. See how an image can be altered and repaired using patch tools such as the clone and heali

events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Engineering with Responsibility. This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.

events.title: Container Gardening: Supporting Our Local Pollinators in Small Spaces

events.title: Photo Editing and Workflow with Adobe Lightroom
events.description: Learn all about Adobe Lightroom CC, an industry standard tool used by photographers to organize, retouch, and share their work. events.title: Introduction to 3D Printing
events.description: Learn how 3D printers work and how you can print at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. events.title: Field of Dreamers: Commemorating the Christie Pits Riots
events.description: CeCe Fortier, scholar and author, brings together organizers of the Field of Dreamers Cooperative Softball Association to commemorate the 93rd anniversary of the Christie Pits riots and discuss what they mean for queer/trans, Black, Jewish, and other communities today. Registration is not required. Drop-in attendance is available until room capacity is reached.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night
events.description: Unleash your creativity as you design your own stunning nail art! Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Tuesday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. *With the exception of June, where we will meet on the 4th Tuesday. This year we are reading:  June 23 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 15 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 17 -  Small Things Like These by Claire Keegan

events.title: Retirement Planning
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop is an introduction to long-term financial planning including retirement savings and better understanding of federal income benefits, such as, Canada Pension Plan (CPP) and Old Age Security (OAS). Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Cercle de Conversation Française / French Conversation Circle
events.description: Nous vous invitons à pratiquer votre français dans un environnement informel ! Tous les niveaux sont bienvenus. We invite you to practice your French in an informal environment! This French Conversation Circle is open to all levels. Accessibilité: Le sous-titrage en direct ou automatique, l’interprétation en langue des signes, les services d’intervenant, ou les services d’accompagnement sont disponibles sur demande. Veuillez soumettre votre demande au moins deux semaines à l’avance à : tpl.ca/ac

events.title: Robotics Workshops for Youth
events.description: Join Upper Toronto Community Robotics for accessible, hands-on robotics and engineering workshops for youth! These programs will help you build foundational skills in engineering design, safety, CAD software, 3D modeling, and introductory robotics coding. This month's theme: Control Systems This program is for youth in grades 6-12. It is highly recommended to attend each session to build on your learning. Please register online.
